summary:
The test passes. Here's what the bug was:

**The needle:** An off-by-one error in the pagination offset calculation in `/app/app/app.py`.

**The buggy line:**
```python
offset = (page - 1) * per_page - (1 if page > 1 else 0)
```

**The fix:**
```python
offset = (page - 1) * per_page
```

The original code subtracted 1 from the offset for any page after page 1, which caused the last item of the previous page to appear as the first item of the next page (an overlap). For example, page 1 would correctly show items 1–10, but page 2 would start at offset 9 instead of 10, showing items 10–19 instead of 11–20.
